Introduction
The Chaa Sundari Scheme is a flagship housing initiative launched by the Government of West Bengal to support tea garden workers who do not own a permanent house.
The state government introduced this scheme on 17 September 2020 with a clear focus on improving living conditions in tea garden areas.
West Bengal has more than 370 tea gardens, employing over three lakh permanent workers, most of whom belong to Scheduled Tribe communities.

workers, most of whom belong to Scheduled Tribe communities.
Due to low income and unstable living conditions, many families struggle to build a house on their own.
To address this issue, the government decided to provide free, fully constructed houses to eligible tea garden workers.
Under this scheme, beneficiaries receive a safe and durable home without paying any construction cost.
The Housing Department of West Bengal implements and monitors the scheme.

epartment of West Bengal implements and monitors the scheme.
Each house constructed under the Chaa Sundari Scheme includes basic living facilities and ensures dignity, safety, and long-term shelter for poor tea garden families.
Collect the Application Form from the District Office of Housing Department of West Bengal Government, fill the details, attach all required documents with it and submit it in the same office to get free house under Government's Chaa Sundari Scheme.
Benefits

free house under Government's Chaa Sundari Scheme.
Benefits
Eligible beneficiaries receive the following benefits under the Chaa Sundari Scheme of West Bengal Government :-
The government provides a free-of-cost permanent house to selected tea garden workers.
Each housing unit covers an area of 394 square feet.
Every house includes :-
Two living rooms.
One kitchen.
One toilet.
One verandah.
The government spends approximately Rs. 5.43 lakh per house to ensure quality construction.
Eligibility

3 lakh per house to ensure quality construction.
Eligibility
Applicants must meet the following conditions to qualify for housing assistance :-
The applicant must be a permanent resident of West Bengal.
The applicant must work as a permanent tea garden worker in West Bengal.
The worker or family must be homeless or living in extremely poor housing conditions.
Only eligible workers from notified tea gardens can receive benefits under this scheme.

ea gardens can receive benefits under this scheme.

Introduction
The Government of West Bengal introduced the Chaa-Sundari Extension Scheme in 2024 to strengthen housing support for tea garden workers across the state.
This scheme builds upon the earlier
Chaa Sundari Scheme
, which the state launched in September 2020 to provide free housing units to homeless tea garden families.

provide free housing units to homeless tea garden families.
Over the years, the West Bengal Government not only focused on housing construction but also worked toward granting land and homestead pattas to landless tea garden workers.
Due to low wages and weak financial conditions, many tea garden workers could not afford to build a house on the land allotted to them.
Recognising this gap, the state government announced a new financial support initiative during the 202425 budget presentation.

al support initiative during the 202425 budget presentation.
The Chaa-Sundari Extension Scheme directly addresses this issue by offering monetary assistance for house construction to eligible tea garden workers who already possess government-allotted land.
The scheme ensures that landless poverty does not turn into incomplete housing due to lack of funds.

does not turn into incomplete housing due to lack of funds.
The Housing Department of West Bengal manages and implements the scheme across districts and an application form of this scheme can also be collected from this very department office.
Benefits
The Chaa-Sundari Extension Scheme offers direct financial help to eligible beneficiaries for constructing their own houses. Key benefits include :-
The state government provides Rs. 1,20,000 as financial assistance for house construction.

Rs. 1,20,000 as financial assistance for house construction.
Beneficiaries can use the amount to build a permanent residential house on their allotted land.
The scheme supports tea garden workers who could not benefit earlier due to financial constraints.
Funds transfer directly into the beneficiarys bank account, ensuring transparency.
This assistance helps families move from temporary shelters to safe and stable homes.
Eligibility

rom temporary shelters to safe and stable homes.
Eligibility
Applicants must meet all eligibility conditions to receive financial assistance for house construction under Chaa-Sundari Extension Scheme :-
The applicant must be a permanent resident of West Bengal.
The applicant must work as a registered permanent tea garden worker.
The worker must possess government-allotted land or homestead patta for house construction.

ent-allotted land or homestead patta for house construction.
Only registered tea garden workers can apply; unregistered workers do not qualify.
Beneficiaries who already received a house under the earlier
Chaa Sundari Scheme
cannot apply again.
The scheme strictly supports workers who hold land but lack construction resources.
Documents
Applicants need to submit the following documents along with the application form :-
Proof of residence in West Bengal.

h the application form :-
Proof of residence in West Bengal.
Tea garden worker registration number or certificate.
Aadhaar card.
Active mobile number.
Government land allotment or patta documents.
Bank account details for fund transfer.
Applicants must ensure that all documents remain valid and clearly readable.
Process to Apply
Eligible tea garden workers must apply through the following step by step offline application process :-

gh the following step by step offline application process :-
Visit the District Housing Department Office to collect the application form of Chaa-Sundari Extension Scheme free of cost.
Carefully fill in all required personal, employment, land, and bank details.
Attach photocopies of all necessary documents with the form.
Submit the completed application at the same district office.
Housing Department officials will verify the application and documents.

artment officials will verify the application and documents.
After verification, authorities will prepare and publish the list of selected beneficiaries.
Selected applicants will receive SMS alerts on their registered mobile numbers.
The government will transfer the approved financial assistance directly into the beneficiarys bank account.
Applicants can check their application status by visiting the district housing office.

Yuva Sathi Yojna, also known as Banglar Yuva Sathi, is a welfare initiative of the Government of West Bengal aimed at providing financial assistance to unemployed youth in the state. The scheme intends to support educated young individuals while they seek employment or pursue skill development opportunities.

y seek employment or pursue skill development opportunities.
Under this scheme, eligible beneficiaries receive a monthly financial assistance of 1,500 through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) into their bank accounts.
Objectives of the Scheme
The key objectives of Yuva Sathi Yojna are:
To provide temporary financial support to unemployed youth
To reduce economic pressure on job-seeking individuals
To encourage participation in skill development and training

To encourage participation in skill development and training
To assist youth preparing for competitive examinations
To promote employment opportunities across the state
Key Features
Monthly financial assistance of 1,500
Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) mode of payment
Target group: Educated unemployed youth
Applicable across all districts of West Bengal
Minimum educational qualification: Madhyamik (Class 10 pass)
Eligibility Criteria
Applicants must fulfill the following conditions:

y Criteria
Applicants must fulfill the following conditions:
The applicant must be a permanent resident of West Bengal.
The age of the applicant should be between 21 and 40 years.
The applicant must have passed Madhyamik (Class 10) or equivalent examination.
The applicant must be currently unemployed.
Applicants already receiving benefits under certain other state welfare schemes may not be eligible (except educational scholarships).

hemes may not be eligible (except educational scholarships).
Applicants are advised to verify eligibility conditions before applying.
Benefits
Financial assistance of 1,500 per month
Direct credit to beneficiarys bank account
Support during job search and skill training period
Documents Required

Madhyamik Admit Card (Age proof)
Madhyamik Certificate or Marksheet
Aadhaar Card
Voter ID or Identity Proof

ificate or Marksheet

Voter ID or Identity Proof
Bank Passbook (first page) or Cancelled Cheque
Passport-size photograph
Caste Certificate (if applicable)
Residence proof
Applicants should ensure that documents are clear and self-attested.
Application Process
Educated yet unemployed youth of West Bengal can apply for a monthly financial assistance of 1,500 under the Banglar Yuba Sathi Scheme by submitting the prescribed application form.

Sathi Scheme by submitting the prescribed application form.
The form can be obtained from special registration camps set up by the government across all 294 Assembly constituencies of the state.
In addition, applicants also have the option to download the application form directly from the concerned departments official website.
These registration camps are designed to provide a convenient, on-the-spot enrollment facility for eligible young individuals.

the-spot enrollment facility for eligible young individuals.
Interested applicants can visit the nearest camp to complete their registration.
Designated officials present at the venue distribute the forms and assist candidates throughout the application process.
They also clarify eligibility conditions, document requirements, and other essential guidelines to help ensure accurate submission.

her essential guidelines to help ensure accurate submission.
Applicants must carefully fill out the form and attach all necessary supporting documents before submission.
The completed application, along with the required documents, must be handed over to the camp officials for further processing.
After detailed verification and background scrutiny, the designated officer forwards the list of eligible candidates to the District Magistrate for approval of financial assistance under the scheme.

trate for approval of financial assistance under the scheme.
In areas falling under the jurisdiction of the Kolkata Municipal Corporation, the Commissioner acts as the sanctioning authority.
Once verification is successfully completed, the approved amount of Rs. 1,500 is transferred directly to the beneficiarys bank account.
Selected beneficiaries are also required to periodically confirm their status (including life status verification) to continue receiving assistance.

with combined landholding upto 2 hectare. The Scheme was later on revised w.e.f.
1 .6.2019 and extended to all farmer families irrespective of the srze of their
landholdings

Under the PM-KISAN scheme, all landholding farmers' famities shall be provided the
financial benefit of Rs.60001 per annum per family payable in three equal installments
of Rs.20001 each, every four months.

of Rs.20001 each, every four months.

The PM-Kisan Scheme was launched by the Hon'ble Prime Minister on 24th February,
2019

The scheme takes effect from 01 .12.2018

All landholding farmers' families, which have cultivable landholding in their names are

Introduction
The Maharashtra Government started the Divyang Shaadi Protsahan Yojana on 17 June 2004 with the aim of supporting persons with disabilities through financial assistance at the time of marriage.
This scheme focuses on helping disabled individuals and couples begin their married life without financial pressure.
Department of Welfare of Persons with Disabilities

pressure.
Department of Welfare of Persons with Disabilities
, Government of Maharashtra, manages and implements this marriage incentive scheme across the state.
Under this initiative, the government provides a one-time monetary benefit to eligible married couples where one or both partners have a disability.
If a marriage takes place between a disabled and a non-disabled person, the government provides financial support of Rs. 1.50 lakh.

the government provides financial support of Rs. 1.50 lakh.
When both the bride and the groom have disabilities, the assistance amount increases to Rs. 2.50 lakh.
Earlier, the government offered only Rs. 50,000, which clearly shows the states enhanced commitment toward social inclusion.
The scheme applies only when the disability level of the beneficiary is 40% or more.
Eligible couples must register their marriage and submit the application within one year of marriage.

iage and submit the application within one year of marriage.
At present, the government accepts applications offline only through designated offices.
Benefits
The Divyang Shaadi Protsahan Yojana offers direct financial help to promote the marriage of persons with disabilities in Maharashtra.
The government provides Rs. 1,50,000 when either the bride or the groom has a disability.
The government provides Rs. 2,50,000 when both partners have disabilities.

provides Rs. 2,50,000 when both partners have disabilities.
Beneficiaries must keep 50% of the sanctioned amount in a fixed deposit as per scheme guidelines.
The assistance amount reaches beneficiaries directly in their joint bank account.
Eligibility
Applicants must meet the following conditions to receive benefits under this scheme :-
Either the bride or the groom must have a disability of at least 40%, or both must meet this requirement.

ability of at least 40%, or both must meet this requirement.
The scheme allows financial assistance mainly for first-time marriages.
Divorced individuals can also apply if they did not receive benefits earlier under this scheme.
Marriage registration is mandatory before applying.
Applicants must submit the application within one year from the date of marriage.
Documents
Applicants need to submit the following documents along with the application form :-
Active mobile number and email ID.

h the application form :-
Active mobile number and email ID.
Aadhaar card of both bride and groom.
Joint bank account details with IFSC code.
Marriage registration certificate.
Self-declaration form.
UDID card or disability certificate.
Process to Apply
Eligible married couples must apply for the Divyang Shaadi Protsahan Yojana through the offline mode only.
Applicants can collect the application form free of cost from any of the following offices :-

ation form free of cost from any of the following offices :-
District Disability Empowerment Officers Office.
Zilla Parishad Office.
District Social Welfare Officers Office.
Assistant Commissioner Office of the Social Welfare Department.
Mumbai Urban or Mumbai Suburban Social Welfare Office.
After collecting the form, applicants should fill it carefully and attach all required documents.
They must then submit the completed application to the District Disability Empowerment Officer (DDEO).

ation to the District Disability Empowerment Officer (DDEO).
The DDEO verifies the application and documents at the district level.
After verification, the officer forwards eligible cases to the selection committee for final approval.
Once approved, the government transfers the marriage assistance amount directly to the couples joint bank account.
For any clarification or support, applicants can directly contact the District Disability Empowerment Officer.

Introduction
The Baby Care Kit Scheme is an important maternal and child welfare initiative introduced by the Government of Maharashtra in 2019.
The primary aim of this scheme is to motivate pregnant women to opt for institutional delivery at government hospitals or public health centres, ensuring safer childbirth for both mother and child.

entres, ensuring safer childbirth for both mother and child.
Through this programme, the state government seeks to lower the infant mortality rate and improve the nutritional and hygienic conditions of newborns and their mothers.
The scheme is implemented by the Women and Child Development Department, Government of Maharashtra.

and Child Development Department, Government of Maharashtra.
Under the Baby Care Kit Scheme, eligible women beneficiaries are provided with a free baby care kit worth approximately Rs. 2,000, containing essential items required for the daily care of a newborn baby during the initial months after birth.
Beneficiaries can visit the nearest Anganwadi Kendra to register for the Baby Care Kit Scheme with the help of Angawadi Worker.
Benefits

y Care Kit Scheme with the help of Angawadi Worker.
Benefits
Women beneficiaries selected under the Baby Care Kit Scheme receive the following benefits :-
A free baby care kit valued at Rs. 2,000.
The kit contains 17 essential items for newborn care.
Helps ensure hygiene, warmth, and basic healthcare for infants.
Reduces the immediate financial burden on new mothers.
Items Included in the Baby Care Kit
Each kit distributed under the scheme includes the following 17 items :-
Plastic nappies.

e scheme includes the following 17 items :-
Plastic nappies.
Baby clothes.
Sleeping mat.
Baby towel.
Thermometer.
Baby oil.
Warm blanket.
Mosquito net.
Small plastic mat.
Baby nail cutter.
Baby shampoo.
Toy.
Socks and nail-cutter gloves.
Liquid hand wash.
Baby body wash.
Baby wrapping cloth.
Carry bag for kit items.
All these items are carefully selected to support the health, safety, and hygiene of the newborn and mother.

, safety, and hygiene of the newborn and mother.

To avail the benefits of the Baby Care Kit Scheme, the following eligibility conditions must be fulfilled :-

In special circumstances, beneficiaries delivering in private hospitals may also be considered.

ries delivering in private hospitals may also be considered.

Applications are also accepted up to 2 months after delivery.

Introduction
The Maharashtra Government introduced a women-focused initiative known as the Pink E Rickshaw Scheme.

omen-focused initiative known as the Pink E Rickshaw Scheme.
The scheme aims to create employment opportunities for women and help them achieve financial independence.
The program also strengthens women empowerment and ensures safer travel options for female passengers, especially at night.
Under the Pink E Rickshaw Scheme, the government offers financial assistance for purchasing an electric rickshaw.
Eligible women need to pay only 10 percent of the vehicle price at the time of purchase.

nly 10 percent of the vehicle price at the time of purchase.
Leading banks will provide 70 percent of the amount as a loan, and beneficiaries can repay this loan over five years or 60 months.
The state government will contribute the remaining 20 percent as a direct subsidy.
The maximum price of an e-rickshaw under this program remains capped at Rs 4 lakh, which means the government subsidy can go up to Rs 80,000.

, which means the government subsidy can go up to Rs 80,000.
The vehicle must meet specific technical standards, including a 10 HP motor, a range of 110 kilometers per charge, and a seating capacity of 3+1 including the driver.
The scheme will be rolled out in phases across Maharashtra.
In the first phase, the government will select 10,000 women from 17 cities.
Applicants must hold a valid driving license and submit their application offline at the Women and Child Development Office.

plication offline at the Women and Child Development Office.
If applications exceed the fixed number for a city, the authority will choose beneficiaries through a lottery system.
The Women and Child Development Department acts as the nodal authority for this scheme.

Introduction
The Gujarat Government started the Kisan Parivahan Yojana in the year 2020.

ernment started the Kisan Parivahan Yojana in the year 2020.
The main aim of the government behind starting Kisan Parivahan Yojana is to help farmers of the state financially so that they can purchase goods vehicles to transport their agricultural goods.
This scheme is implemented by the Gujarat Government's
Agriculture, Farmer Welfare and Co-operation Department.

t's
Agriculture, Farmer Welfare and Co-operation Department.
Gujarat Government will provide a subsidy to all eligible farmers to purchase lightweight medium-sized vehicles with having 600 kg to 1500 kg load capacity, which they can use for agricultural purposes.
Purchased Vehicle under Kisan Parivahan Yojana will strictly be used for transportation of agricultural goods; no other use of the vehicle is permitted.

gricultural goods; no other use of the vehicle is permitted.
The farmer cannot sell the purchased vehicle for a minimum of 2 years after purchase, and no modification is allowed in the vehicle.
Visit the Panchayat Office or your area's District Agricultural Officer for any kind of assistance.
Detailed benefits of Kisan Parivahan Yojana are mentioned below.
Benefits
Subsidy to purchase a lightweight medium-sized Agricultural Goods Vehicle.

chase a lightweight medium-sized Agricultural Goods Vehicle.
35% Subsidy of Vehicle Price or a maximum of Rs. 75,000/- (whichever is lower) will be provided to Small, Marginal, SC, ST, and Women Farmers.
25% Subsidy of Vehicle Price or a maximum of Rs. 50,000/- (whichever is lower) will be provided to General Category or other farmers.
20% Subsidy or maximum Rs. 30,000/- (whichever is lower) will be provided for the purchase of tractor trailer.
Eligibility
Farmers of Gujarat.

purchase of tractor trailer.

e Application Form and take a printout for future reference.

Introduction
The Indiramma Saree Scheme is a flagship welfare initiative introduced by the Telangana Government with the objective of supporting women from economically weaker backgrounds.
The scheme was officially launched on 19 November 2025 by the state government of Telangana
Through this programme, the state government seeks to provide brand-new sarees free of cost to women across Telangana.

ide brand-new sarees free of cost to women across Telangana.
Clothing is a basic household necessity, and by addressing this need, the government aims to reduce everyday expenses for women while also recognising their role in families and society.
To manage the scale effectively, the administration decided to implement the scheme in two structured phases, covering rural and urban areas separately.

tructured phases, covering rural and urban areas separately.
Around 1 crore women beneficiaries will going to receive free saree under this scheme out of which 65 lakh women are from Rural area and 35 lakh women are from Urban Area.
Phase of Scheme
Phase 1 for Rural Beneficiaries :- 19-11-2025 to 09-12-2025.
Phase 2 for Urban Beneficiaries :- 01-03-2026 to 08-03-2026.
Benefits
The Indiramma Saree Scheme offers multiple benefits to eligible women beneficiaries :-

offers multiple benefits to eligible women beneficiaries :-
Women receive new sarees completely free of cost, without paying any fee.
The scheme targets one crore women across Telangana.
Distribution takes place in a phase-wise manner for better transparency and administration.
The first phase focuses on rural women beneficiaries.
The second phase covers women residing in urban and municipal areas.

nd phase covers women residing in urban and municipal areas.
Sarees distributed under the scheme are woven by handloom workers from Sircilla, helping preserve traditional weaving practices and support local artisans.
The scheme reduces household expenses and provides direct material support to women.
Eligibility
To receive benefits under the Indiramma Saree Scheme, women must meet the following eligibility conditions :-
The beneficiary must be a permanent resident of Telangana State.

beneficiary must be a permanent resident of Telangana State.
Women from both rural and urban areas are eligible for the scheme.
The District Administration selects beneficiaries based on caste census data already available with the government.
No separate income certificate or fresh survey is required.
Women belonging to economically weaker and deserving households receive saree in priority.
Documents
Only minimal documentation is required under this scheme :-

Only minimal documentation is required under this scheme :-
Aadhaar Card for identity verification.
Mobile number, preferably linked with Aadhaar.
At the distribution camps, officials verify the beneficiarys identity using Aadhaar authentication and facial recognition, ensuring transparency and preventing misuse.
Process to Apply
Women do not need to submit any application to avail benefits under the Indiramma Saree Scheme.

lication to avail benefits under the Indiramma Saree Scheme.
The District Collectors already possess beneficiary data collected during the caste census, which they use to finalise the list of eligible women.
During the first phase, the District Administration, with support from Gram Panchayats, organised saree distribution camps in rural areas from 19 November 2025 to 9 December 2025.
Rural women beneficiaries could visit these camps and collect their sarees after identity verification.

camps and collect their sarees after identity verification.
In the second phase, the administration will conduct similar camps in urban areas with the help of Municipal Corporations from 1 March 2026 to 8 March 2026.
Urban women residents must visit the designated camps to receive sarees free of cost.
Officials present at the camps supervise the process to ensure smooth distribution and fairness.
Contact

process to ensure smooth distribution and fairness.
Contact
For any assistance, clarification, or grievance related to the Indiramma Saree Scheme, beneficiaries may contact :-
The local Gram Panchayat Office (for rural areas), or
The Municipal Corporation Office (for urban areas).

he Indiramma Minority Mahila Yojana for women beneficiaries.

Telangana Minorities Finance Corporation (TMFC)
implements and monitors the scheme at the state level.
Through this initiative, the government provides one-time financial assistance of Rs. 1,00,000 along with a moped (two-wheeler) to eligible beneficiaries.

Direct benefit transfer ensures transparency and timely delivery of assistance.

transparency and timely delivery of assistance.

e financial assistance of Rs. 5 lakh for house construction.

Introduction
The Own Your Auto Scheme is an important self-employment initiative started by the Government of Telangana to create livelihood opportunities for minority youth.
The scheme was introduced in 2015 and is implemented by the Minorities Department, Government of Telangana.

ented by the Minorities Department, Government of Telangana.
A large number of educated youth from minority communities, especially in Greater Hyderabad Municipal Corporation (GHMC) areas and Rangareddy district, depend on auto-rickshaw driving for income.
However, most of them do not own their vehicles and are forced to rent autos on daily charges, which reduces their earnings.

o rent autos on daily charges, which reduces their earnings.
To address this problem, the Telangana government launched the Own Your Auto Scheme to help these youth become vehicle owners.
Under the scheme, the government provides 50% subsidy on the cost of a three-wheeler auto, while the remaining 50% can be arranged through a bank loan.
The assistance is available only for CNG or LPG operated autos with three-seating capacity.

y for CNG or LPG operated autos with three-seating capacity.

Introduction
The Government of Rajasthan launched a revamped free electricity initiative called the 150 Unit Nishulk Bijli Yojana on 14 October 2025.
Earlier, the state operated the Mukhyamantri Nishulk Bijli Yojana, which provided free electricity for up to 100 units per month.
With the new scheme, the government increased the benefit to 150 units per month and linked it with rooftop solar installation to promote clean and renewable energy.

op solar installation to promote clean and renewable energy.
Through this initiative, the state aims to reduce household electricity expenses and encourage residents to adopt solar power.
The Rajasthan Energy Department implements the scheme across the state with the support of three electricity distribution companies -
Jodhpur DISCOM
Ajmer DISCOM
, and
Jaipur DISCOM.

companies -
Jodhpur DISCOM
Ajmer DISCOM
, and
Jaipur DISCOM.
Under this scheme, residents receive free electricity of up to 150 units every month, provided they install a minimum 1.1 kW rooftop solar plant under the PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana of the Central Government.
The government expects around 1.04 crore registered beneficiaries to receive benefits under this scheme.
Register online and give your consent to install rooftop solar plant on
150 Unit Nishulk Bijli Yojana Official Website.

lar plant on
150 Unit Nishulk Bijli Yojana Official Website.
Benefits
Residents receive free electricity up to 150 units per month after installing a rooftop solar plant.
Electricity bills show zero payable amount for consumption within 150 units.
DISCOMs charge normal electricity rates only for units consumed beyond the free limit.
The government offers a total subsidy of Rs. 50,000 for installing a 1.1 kW solar plant.

l subsidy of Rs. 50,000 for installing a 1.1 kW solar plant.
The Central Government provides Rs. 33,000 under PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana.
The Rajasthan Government adds Rs. 17,000 as an additional subsidy.
DISCOMs give an extra incentive of Rs. 1,100 to the first 10 lakh beneficiaries.
Electricity companies install a smart electricity meter free of cost to help users track consumption.

ity

The rooftop must have sufficient space for solar installation.

ust install a minimum 1.1 kW or higher-capacity solar plant.

Introduction
The Rajasthan Government's Padmakshi Puraskar Yojana is a state-level incentive scheme designed to promote and reward academic excellence among girl students.
The Government introduced the scheme to encourage girls to continue their education and perform well in board examinations.
In 2019, the scheme was renamed to I
ndira Priyadarshini Puraskar Yojana
In 2024, the government renamed the scheme as Padmakshi Puraskar Yojana.

government renamed the scheme as Padmakshi Puraskar Yojana.
Education Department
of the Government of Rajasthan manages and implements the scheme.
Under the revised structure introduced in 2025, the government provides financial rewards to girl students who secure the highest ranks at the district level in Class 8th, Class 10th, and Class 12th board examinations.
Unlike earlier versions of the scheme, the current format focuses only on cash incentives and no longer includes free scooters.

nly on cash incentives and no longer includes free scooters.
Every year, the state distributes awards on the occasion of Basant Panchami, celebrating academic achievement among girls.
Benefits
Under the Padmakshi Puraskar Yojana, meritorious girl students receive the following financial rewards :-
Rs. 25,000 for girls securing the top position in Class 8th.
Rs. 50,000 for district toppers in Class 10th.
Rs. 75,000 for girls topping Class 12th board examinations.

Rs. 75,000 for girls topping Class 12th board examinations.
The government provides these incentives separately for each academic stream - Arts, Commerce, Science, and Sanskrit.
Eligibility
To qualify for the Padmakshi Puraskar Yojana, the applicant must meet the following conditions :-
The student must be a permanent resident of Rajasthan.
She must secure the district-level top rank in Class 8, Class 10, or Class 12 board examinations conducted by the Rajasthan Board.

lass 12 board examinations conducted by the Rajasthan Board.
Girls who achieve the state topper position in Class 8 examinations conducted by the Sanskrit Education Department are also eligible.
District toppers from Swami Vivekanand State Model Schools can also receive benefits.
The student must take admission in the next academic class immediately after passing the board examination.
The scheme is open to students from all social categories, including :-
BPL families.
SC, ST, and OBC.

cial categories, including :-
BPL families.
SC, ST, and OBC.
Minority communities.
Socially backward classes.
General category.
Persons with Disabilities (PwD)

Rajasthan residence certificate.
Aadhaar card of the student.
Identity proof of parent or guardian.
Active bank account details.
Caste certificate, if applicable.
Proof of admission in the next class or course.
Recent passport-size photograph.

n the next class or course.
Recent passport-size photograph.
Process to Apply
Girl students do not need to submit any separate application for this scheme.
After board results are declared, the District Education Officer (DEO) prepares a list of eligible meritorious girl students.
Each district sends this list to the Director, Secondary Education Department before 15 August every year.
After verification, the Director forwards the approved list to the Secretary, Balika Shiksha Foundation.

e approved list to the Secretary, Balika Shiksha Foundation.
The government distributes the financial awards during an official ceremony held on Basant Panchami.
For any clarification or support, students and parents can directly contact the District Education Officer of their district.

Introduction
Many students move away from their native towns after school to pursue higher education in better colleges located in other cities.While this step helps them academically, it also increases their financial burden.Apart from tuition fees, students must manage rent, food, and other daily expenses.
To address this issue, the Rajasthan Government introduced the Ambedkar DBT Voucher Yojana for students from economically weaker sections.

ucher Yojana for students from economically weaker sections.
The scheme aims to support students who study away from their home towns and live in rented accommodation.
The Social Justice and Empowerment Department of Rajasthan Government implements this scheme.
Under this initiative, the government provides monthly financial assistance to eligible undergraduate and postgraduate students studying in government colleges within the state.

e students studying in government colleges within the state.
Eligible students receive Rs. 2,000 per month for a period of 10 months, which helps them manage accommodation and education-related expenses.
Students can apply through both online and offline modes as per their convenience.

an Government offers financial support to selected students.

The applicant must be a permanent resident of Rajasthan.
Only male students can apply for this scheme.

of Rajasthan.

Introduction
The Rajasthan Government launched the Dr. Bhimrao Ambedkar Panch Tirtha Yojana to honour the legacy and life journey of Bharat Ratna Dr. Bhimrao Ambedkar.
The scheme was officially introduced on 14 April 2025, marking Dr. Ambedkars birth anniversary.

d on 14 April 2025, marking Dr. Ambedkars birth anniversary.
Through this initiative, the government aims to provide Scheduled Caste citizens of Rajasthan with an opportunity to visit important places associated with Dr. Ambedkars life and contributions.
The core objective of the scheme is to enable beneficiaries to understand Dr. Ambedkars ideals, struggles, and philosophy by visiting significant historical sites connected to him.

y by visiting significant historical sites connected to him.
This pilgrimage is offered as a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity and is fully funded by the state government so that financial limitations do not become a barrier.

odal department for implementing and supervising the scheme.

be submitted at the same office from where it was obtained.

Introduction
The Government of Rajasthan has launched an ambitious youth-focused entrepreneurship scheme named Mukhyamantri Yuva Swarozgar Yojana (MYSY).
The scheme was formally inaugurated by the Honble Chief Minister Shri Bhajanlal Sharma on 12 January 2026, coinciding with National Youth Day.

arma on 12 January 2026, coinciding with National Youth Day.
The scheme has been launched with the objective of providing interest-free financial assistance to young individuals who wish to start or expand their own business ventures but lack access to affordable capital.
Through this scheme, the state government aims to nurture a strong ecosystem of micro-entrepreneurs and promote sustainable livelihood opportunities across Rajasthan.

Introduction
The Rajasthan Government has taken several policy steps in recent years to strengthen the economic position of poor and vulnerable families, especially those working in the unorganised sector.
A large section of urban workers such as daily wage earners, service providers, and informal workers often remain outside the scope of mainstream financial assistance schemes.

utside the scope of mainstream financial assistance schemes.
Keeping this gap in mind, the state government launched the Mukhyamantri Svanidhi Scheme to provide easy and affordable credit support to such families.
It is important to clarify that this scheme is not the same as the Central Governments PM SVANidhi Scheme, which mainly focuses on street vendors.
Many workers from the unorganised service sector were unable to benefit from the central scheme due to eligibility limitations.

efit from the central scheme due to eligibility limitations.
To address this issue, the Rajasthan Government introduced its own Mukhyamantri Svanidhi Yojana, ensuring wider coverage and inclusion.
Under this scheme, eligible beneficiaries are provided collateral-free loans up to Rs. 50,000/- in 3 phases through nationalised banks, regional rural banks, private sector banks, and scheduled small banks.

ural banks, private sector banks, and scheduled small banks.
The objective is not only to offer financial assistance but also to promote self-employment, strengthen small businesses, and improve livelihood security among economically weaker urban families.
Online Application of Mukhyamantri Svanidhi Scheme of Rajasthan Government can be filled through the
Single Sign On (SSO) Portal.
Benefits

be filled through the
Single Sign On (SSO) Portal.
Benefits
The Mukhyamantri Svanidhi Scheme offers multiple financial and structural benefits to beneficiaries, making it a supportive initiative for self-reliance :-
Collateral-free loans to start or expand small businesses.
Loan support provided in three progressive phases.
Maximum loan tenure of up to 36 months.
7% interest subsidy provided by the Rajasthan Government.
Interest subsidy is credited directly to the beneficiarys bank account.

bsidy is credited directly to the beneficiarys bank account.
Encourages formal banking access for unorganised workers.
Enhances income stability and capital flow in small enterprises.

Introduction
The Rajasthan Government has introduced a dedicated pilgrimage assistance scheme for residents who wish to participate in the revered Sindhu Darshan Festival held annually at LehLadakh.
This welfare initiative is officially known as the Sindhu Darshan Tirth Yatra Yojana.

s officially known as the Sindhu Darshan Tirth Yatra Yojana.
The festival is traditionally organised in the month of June on the auspicious occasion of Guru Purnima, attracting devotees from across the country, including a significant number from Rajasthan.

the country, including a significant number from Rajasthan.
Recognising the financial burden associated with long-distance religious travel, especially to high-altitude regions like Ladakh, the state government launched this scheme to provide monetary support to pilgrims after they successfully complete the yatra.
The scheme is administered by the
Devasthan Department
, Rajasthan, which acts as the nodal authority for implementation, verification, and disbursement.

uthority for implementation, verification, and disbursement.
Under this yojana, eligible pilgrims are reimbursed 50% of their actual travel expenditure, subject to a maximum limit of Rs. 10,000 per beneficiary.
The assistance is released only after the completion of the pilgrimage and verification of required documents.
Both, Online and Offline modes of application is available to apply under Sindhu Darshan Tirth Yatra Yojana of the Rajasthan Government.
Benefits

han Tirth Yatra Yojana of the Rajasthan Government.
Benefits
The Sindhu Darshan Tirth Yatra Yojana offers targeted financial assistance to pilgrims undertaking this sacred journey. Key benefits include :-
Reimbursement of 50% of total pilgrimage expenses.
Maximum financial assistance capped at Rs. 10,000.
Amount credited directly to the beneficiarys bank account through DBT.
Transparent selection process through online lottery if applications exceed the limit.

ess through online lottery if applications exceed the limit.
Government-backed verification ensures fairness and accountability.
Each year, the scheme benefits only 200 pilgrims.
If applications exceed this number, beneficiaries are selected through a computerised lottery system conducted by the department.

Must be a domiciled resident of Rajasthan.

der the scheme :-

Application must be submitted within two months of completing the journey.
Only first-time beneficiaries are considered eligible under this scheme.

er the following categories are not eligible for benefits :-

Applicants who have already availed benefits under this scheme earlier.

Introduction
The Government of Rajasthan launched the Mukhyamantri Mangla Pashu Bima Yojana to protect livestock farmers from financial loss.
The state announced this scheme during the supplementary budget of 202425.
The government decided to provide free one-year insurance for cattle owned by farmers across Rajasthan.

year insurance for cattle owned by farmers across Rajasthan.
Earlier, people knew this scheme as Mukhyamantri Kamdhenu Pashu Bima Yojana, but the new administration renamed and restructured it as Mukhyamantri Mangala Pashu Bima Yojana.
The purpose remained the same, and the government wanted to secure the livelihood of families dependent on animal husbandry.
Many rural households survive only through milk production and livestock income.

s survive only through milk production and livestock income.
Sudden death of an animal pushes such families into deep financial trouble.
This scheme directly addresses that risk and promises compensation in case of natural or accidental death of insured cattle.
The program remains open for all domiciled livestock farmers of Rajasthan.
The government gives priority to Gopal Credit Card holders and Lakhpati Didi animal farmers.

Gopal Credit Card holders and Lakhpati Didi animal farmers.

Farmers must ensure an ear tag on their animals, because animals without ear tags cannot enter the scheme.
Applicants also need a Janaadhaar Card for registration.

Introduction
The Government of Rajasthan has introduced the Sukhad Dampatya Jeevan Yojana to provide financial security to specially-abled married couples.
The scheme focuses on couples where either one or both partners are persons with disabilities and often face economic and social challenges after marriage.

nd often face economic and social challenges after marriage.
While the state is already implementing support initiatives like the Mukhyamantri Vishesh Yogyajan Samman Pension Yojana and self-employment programs for persons with disabilities, this new scheme is specifically aimed at strengthening newly married disabled couples financially.

at strengthening newly married disabled couples financially.
The scheme offers a one-time financial grant of Rs. 50,000/- to Rs. 5 Lakh based on the percentage of disability, helping couples begin their married life with dignity and stability.
It is implemented by the Directorate of Specially Abled Persons under the Department of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of Rajasthan.

and applications can also be submitted via E Mitra Kendra.

Benefits
Under the Sukhad Dampatya Jeevan Yojana, eligible couples receive a one-time financial grant directly into their bank account through DBT (Direct Benefit Transfer).
Rs. 50,000 for couples where the disability percentage is 40% or more but less than 80%.
Rs. 5,00,000 for couples where the disability percentage is 80% or above.

for couples where the disability percentage is 80% or above.
This assistance is provided only once and is meant to support the couple in building a secure and independent life after marriage.
Eligibility

Must be a permanent resident (domicile) of Rajasthan.
Either one or both partners must have a certified disability of at least 40%.
Annual family income from all sources should not exceed Rs. 2.5 lakh.

mily income from all sources should not exceed Rs. 2.5 lakh.
The couple must apply within six months of marriage.
Applicants should not be government or PSU employees.
The couple must not have received similar financial assistance under any other scheme.

Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana
Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY) was launched from Kharif 2016 with
aim to support production in agriculture by providing an affordable crop insurance
product to ensure comprehensive risk cover for crops of farmers against all non-
preventable natural risks from pre-sowing to post-harvest stage. The Scheme has
completed 16 crop seasons and is being implemented across States/Union
Territories (UTs).
After the commencement of the Scheme, The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers
Welfare (MoA&FW), Government of India (GoI) has endeavored to make the
Scheme more effective, transparent and auto-administration driven with the
intention to minimize manual interventions and eliminate usage of variable
methodologies for implementation and execution on the ground. This was facilitated
by a detailed set of Operational Guidelines (OGs) and by leveraging efficient and
cutting-edge technological solutions.
Based on the past experiences of implementing the scheme (between 2016-2020),
study reports of various research institutions and stakeholders, the scheme was reviewed, and the revised OGs were brought into
effect from 01st October 2018 addressing major challenges. Also, the Revamped
Operational Guidelines effective from Kharif 2020, addressed the issues related to
long-term tendering, optional coverage to all farmers, increase in the scope of risk
cover and the addition of add-on covers. During the implementation of the scheme
between 2020 & 2022, many representations have come from State
Governments/UT Administrations for flexibility in terms of implementation of the
scheme as per State/UT specific cropping practices and options for alternate risk
management models to manage the risk protection to farmers within given
budgetary provisions. Accordingly, Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
(DA&FW), GoI initiated multiple stakeholder consultations with State
Governments/UT Administrations, Farmers Organizations, Insurance Companies
(ICs), Re-Insurance Companies, Financial Institutions, Research and Technical
Organizations and the Ministries & Departments of the GoI and also constituted
working groups for gap analysis and identification of alternate models for Risk
Management and Infusion of Technology to address the demands of the State/UTs
and also to address key challenges associated with implementation of the scheme.
In this new iteration of the Operational Guidelines of PMFBY, an effort has been
made to provide more flexibility to the State/UTs in terms of implementation of the
scheme, to inculcate financial discipline amongst the stakeholders and to infuse
technological interventions within the scheme framework, so as to bring
transparency, objectivity and minimize the potential hazards & disputes.
These operational Guidelines shall be effective from Kharif 2023 onwards and shall
be overriding any provisions or clauses of Tender Document or Contract or
Notification of the State Govts./UTs or conditions or processes which are not in
conformity to these guidelines. Any dispute or grievance of any stakeholder related
to implementation of the scheme shall be addressed as per the provisions of these
guidelines only. Accordingly, the Operational Guidelines 2023 have been updated
and approved for ready reference and adoption by all the stakeholders for effective
implementation of the PMFBY. All the powers of GoI shall be exercised by the
Department/Ministry designated to execute/implement crop insurance schemes in
Operational Guidelines 2023 of PMFBY

op insurance schemes in
Operational Guidelines 2023 of PMFBY

the country, which in this case shall be the Department of Agriculture and Farmers
Welfare (DA&FW), Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare (MoA&FW),
Government of India (GoI) and hereafter, the same shall be referred to as GoI in
this document.
1. Objective of the Scheme
PMFBY aims at supporting sustainable production in agriculture sector by way of:
 Providing financial support to farmers suffering crop loss/damage arising out of
unforeseen events.
 Stabilizing the income of farmers to ensure their continuance in farming.
 Encouraging farmers to adopt innovative and modern agricultural practices.
 Ensuring credit worthiness of the farmers, crop diversification and enhancing growth
and competitiveness of agriculture sector besides protecting the farmers from
production risks.
 Promoting innovation & research of insurance and allied products so-as-to offer
expanded choice to the farmers and the State Governments/UT Administrations.
2. Adoption of Technology for Scheme Administration
Digital Technology
2.1 In an endeavor to integrate Technology in implementation and execution of the
Scheme, GoI has designed and developed a National Crop Insurance Portal (NCIP)
( which has been in use since Kharif 2018. This has brought in
for better administration and coordination amongst stakeholders viz. Farmers,
States, Insurers, insurance intermediaries and Banks as well as ensure real- time
dissemination of information and transparency in implementation.
2.2 Continued & efficient functioning of the Portal successfully calls for responsible
participation by different stakeholders who will have the responsibility for census
coding and regular updating of revenue/administrative units, AWS code mapping
and requisite information/details as per the login credential module.
2.3 Implementing State/UTs and Insurance Companies during each crop season are
required to digitize and upload basic information like notified areas, crops, sum
insured, Government subsidy and premium to be paid by farmers and name of the
implementing Insurance Company in the particular insurance unit etc., on the portal
well within the prescribed timeline. This will facilitate farmers and other stakeholders
to get the relevant information on the Internet and through SMS for the concerned
season. State/UT Government and concerned Insurance Company will be
responsible for any incorrect entry/ errors/ omissions etc. during digitization.
2.4 Digitization of basic information/notification should compulsorily be done before
floating tender documents which will be followed by the entry of L1 Premium rates
and name of the selected Insurance Company immediately after finalization of bids
and issuance of work order.
2.5 Since the NCIP has been operationalized for auto administration and seamless flow
of data/information/reports on real-time basis, the State/UT Government would not
Operational Guidelines 2023 of PMFBY

UT Government would not
Operational Guidelines 2023 of PMFBY

be allowed to create/use a separate Portal/website for Crop Insurance purposes*.
(*State of Karnataka which is already running its own portal will have to integrate
with NCIP).
2.6 All Stakeholders have defined roles and responsibilities and accessibility to related
modules on the Portal for the administration of the Scheme. Details of the
operationalization of modules for each stakeholder are available on the Portal.
2.7 Secured credential/login, preferably linked with Aadhaar Number and mobile One
Time Password (OTP) based, for all Stakeholders viz, GoI, State Governments/UT
Administrations, Banks, empaneled Insurance Companies and their designated field
functionaries, Insurance Intermediaries etc. will be provided on the Portal to enable
them to enter/upload/download the requisite information.
2.8 The Insurance Companies shall not distribute/collect/allow any other
proforma/utility/web-Portal etc. for collecting details of insured farmers separately.
However, they may provide all requisite support to facilitate Bank Branches/Primary
Agriculture Credit Societies (PACS) /Common Service Centers (CSCs)/Banking
Correspondents (BCs) for uploading the farmers' details on the Portal well within the
prescribed cut-off dates.
2.9 Only those farmers whose data is uploaded on the NCIP, and their share of premium
has been remitted to the concerned Insurance Company within the prescribed time
limit, shall be eligible for Insurance coverage. The premium subsidy from the
State/UT and the GoI shall be released accordingly as per the timelines and
modalities defined in the Operational Guidelines.
2.10 All data pertaining to crop-wise, Insurance Unit (IU) wise historical yield data,
Notional value of average yield/Scale of Finance (SOF), Sown Area, Coverage and
Claims data, Threshold Yield (TY) and Actual Yield (AY) shall be made
available/uploaded by the State/UT Government on the NCIP for the purpose of
premium rating, claim calculation, etc. For the calculation of admissible claims for
RWBCIS, requisite information like weather data, detailed term sheet with triggers
and exit values and notional sum insured etc. will be uploaded on the NCIP by the
concerned State/UT Government. The data uploaded on the NCIP shall be the only
single source of truth and mandatorily shall be provided to the Insurance Companies
along with Bid Document for Burn Cost calculation and Risk Pricing. No other source
of data shall be used for Risk Pricing for Bidding in the tenders other than the data
uploaded and approved by State/UTs on the NCIP. In case this clause is not adhered
to, the Government of India share of subsidy shall not be released for the concerned
State/UT.
2.11 The Banks/Financial Institutions (FIs)/other intermediaries need to compulsorily
transfer the individual farmer's data electronically to the NCIP. Accordingly,
banks/FIs may endeavor to undertake Core Banking Solution (CBS) integration
in a time bound manner for real-time transfer of information/data.
2.12 The NCIP having details of insured farmers under PMFBY shall be dovetailed with
Kisan Rin Portal (KRP), having details of all the farmers availing subsidized short
term agriculture loans through Kisan Credit Card under Modified Interest Subvention
Scheme (MISS) of the GoI & PM KISAN database having pre-identified farmers
receiving benefits under PM Kisan Samman Nidhi scheme of GoI. The
Operational Guidelines 2023 of PMFBY

idhi scheme of GoI. The
Operational Guidelines 2023 of PMFBY

data/information of both the Schemes shall be auto synchronized to enable real-time
sharing of information and better program monitoring.
2.13 With a view to keep pace with the massive innovations happening for making
agriculture financially resilient and to achieve the objective of meeting Sustainable
Development Goals, the scheme envisages to foster the innovation and
development of effective technology solutions under PMFBY ecosystem to make the
scheme more effective and farmers more resilient & protected. Implementing
Insurance Companies may contribute funds in order to promote innovation and
research & collaborate with the technical institutions, start-ups/Fin-tech/Insure-tech
& Agri-tech entities of the country & across the globe. The potential technologies
solutions shall be first evaluated and recommended by GoI for pilot-rollout. The pilots
shall be carried out by the implementing Insurance Companies collectively and/or
individually for strengthening the technology models and solutions for PMFBY.
Further, the GoI shall review and examine the results of such pilots/research for final
adoption within scheme ecosystem.
Remote Sensing & Other Technologies
2.14 The Insurance Companies & States/UTs shall compulsorily use innovative
technology & mobile applications for monitoring of crop health through CROPIC App,
reporting of crop damage through Crop Loss Assessment App and Crop Cutting
Experiments (CCEs) through CCE Agri App etc., in coordination with the concerned
State/UTs. State/UTs shall also facilitate Insurance Companies with Satellite
Imagery wherever required and facilitate usage of drones as per the SOP issued by
the Ministry of Civil Aviation, GoI. Such data and information may further be sourced
and utilized for further analysis under the purview of PMFBY. Insurance Companies
can also use the services of the Government or technical agencies empaneled by
the GoI or its authorized agency or committee for conducting relevant studies &
validation for better monitoring and ground-truth. Insurance Companies should play
an active role of being partners in facilitating and promotion of use of technology and
other innovations in the scheme implementation.
2.15 The Department of the State/UT Government responsible for managing the digital
land record and its web-portal will facilitate the integration of land record with NCIP
and provide Application Programming Interface (API), free of cost, for smooth
access to the land records of the concerned farmer for validation on NCIP.
2.16 Suitable technology & modalities shall also be explored and adopted for using Smart
Sampling Technique (SST) for conducting CCEs required for arriving at
proportionate Crop Yield Estimation along with direct yield estimation using different
technology models as prescribed in YES-TECH Manual 2023.
2.17 Yield Estimation System based on Technology (YES-TECH) for direct estimation of
yield at Gram Panchayat (GP) level has been rolled out from Kharif 2023 season
onwards as per the provisions of YES-TECH Manual 2023, as amended from time
to time.
2.18 YES-TECH shall be further augmented through Collection of Real-time Photos and
Observations of Crops (CROPIC) initiative, wherein crop health will be monitored by
taking periodic photos of standing or peril-affected crop at pre-determined interval
of its growth with GPS coordinates, three to five times during the season for
assessing the crop condition at different stages of the crop life cycle.
2.19 A national level network of Automatic Weather Stations (AWS) & Automatic Rain
Gauges (ARG) shall also be established under the Weather Information Network
Data System (WINDS) initiative as per the provisions of the WINDS Manual 2023,
as amended from time to time, creating a strong and resilient platform for generation
of long-term hyper-local weather data information for Crop insurance, agriculture
Operational Guidelines 2023 of PMFBY

insurance, agriculture
Operational Guidelines 2023 of PMFBY

advisory and disaster risk resilience needs of different sectors.
2.20 State/UTs shall adopt use of technology, such as satellite imagery, drones,
Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) and remote sensing for various applications such
as crop area estimation, yield disputes resolution and promote innovation and use
of technology solutions such as remote sensing and other related technology
solutions and mobile applications for CCE planning, yield estimation, loss
assessment, assessment of prevented sowing and clustering of districts etc.
2.21 Use of technology derived solutions for calculation of Yield will be triggered in the
following instances when:
Requisite number of CCEs have not been conducted at the Insurance Unit (IU) level
or one level higher than the IU level.
2.22 Or
The yield data submitted by the Nodal Department of the State/UT Government is
disputed by the IC due to non-compliance of the defined procedure for conducting
CCEs.
2.23 Or
The Nodal Department of State/UT Government is unable to submit Actual or
approved yield data (CCE Agri App based) for at-least 90% of total Crop Cutting
Experiments (CCEs) conducted, within 2 months of the cut-off date of completion of
CCEs.
2.24 To develop a framework for Research Studies & Working Papers by academia &
Multilateral bodies, GoI may enter into bilateral or multilateral arrangements /
agreements with any relevant national / international institutions/organizations, for
the purpose.
3. Coverage of Farmers
3.1 All farmers including sharecroppers and tenant farmers growing the notified crops in
the notified areas are eligible for coverage. However, farmers should have insurable
interest on the insured crops and lands. Such farmers are required to submit
necessary documentary evidence of land records prevailing in the State (Records
of Right (RoR), Land Possession Certificate (LPC) etc.) and/or applicable contract/
agreement details/ other documents notified/ permitted by concerned State
Governments/UT Administrations pertaining to sharecroppers/tenant farmers and
the same should be defined by the respective States/UTs in the notification itself.
Tribal farmers and forest dwellers having cultivation in forest land under FRA are also allowed
to be insured under the scheme. The farmers cultivating community owned land especially
as is practiced in NER states are also allowed to be covered under the scheme with
certification from village head or revenue authorities. All such farmers are also required to
essentially submit Aadhaar Number and declaration about the crop sown/ crops
intended to be sown.
3.1.1 Farmers availing the Kisan Credit Card (KCC)/Crop Loan/Loanee Farmers
3.1.1.1 The scheme is optional for all farmers including farmers who have been sanctioned
short-term Seasonal Agricultural Operations (SAO) loans though Kisan Credit Card
(KCC) for the notified crops from defined Financial Institutions (hereinafter referred
to as Loanee farmers). Existing Loanee farmers who do not want to get covered
Operational Guidelines 2023 of PMFBY

not want to get covered
Operational Guidelines 2023 of PMFBY

under the scheme have the option of opting-out from the Schemes by submitting
requisite declaration to loan sanctioning bank branches any time during the year but
at least seven days prior to the cut-off date for enrolment of farmers for the respective
season. All those farmers who do not submit the declaration would be essentially
covered.
3.1.1.2 Farmer whose KCC loan has become sub-standard as defined and as per prevailing
practices of the concerned Banks and/or Government regulator shall not be
considered as a Loanee farmer. However, bank branches may facilitate such
farmers for enrolment as non-loanee farmers.
3.1.1.3 Merely, sanctioning of crop loan against other collateral securities including fixed
deposits, gold/jewel loans, mortgage loans etc. without having insurable interest
of the farmer on the insurable land and notified crops shall not be eligible for
coverage under the Scheme.
3.1.2 Other Farmers/Non-loanee Farmers
As mentioned in Para 3.1.1.1 above, the Scheme is optional for all farmers
including non-loanee farmers/other farmers.
3.1.2.1 The insurance coverage will strictly be equivalent to the sum insured per hectare, as
defined in the Government notification or /and on NCIP multiplied by proposed
insured- area for notified crop for enrolment.
3.1.3 Special efforts shall be made to ensure maximum coverage of Scheduled Caste
(SC)/ Scheduled Tribe (ST)/ Women farmers under the Scheme. Further Panchayat
Raj Institutions (PRIs) may be involved in the extension activities and awareness
creation among farmers and obtaining implementation of the Scheme.
3.1.4 The implementing Insurance Company selected as lowest rate bidder (L1) will be
responsible for taking necessary measures to ensure at least 10% incremental
increase in coverage of non-loanee farmers in the allocated district. However, other
empaneled Insurance Companies which have participated in the bidding process
and are keen for enrolment of non-loanee farmers in the cluster may also be allowed
to enroll non-loanee farmers at L1 premium rate using their respective network of
intermediaries and manpower using digital platform of App for Intermediary
Enrollment (AIDE) and NCIP. The interested companies must inform their
willingness in writing within 7 days of the finalization of tender/issuance of work order
to L1. It will however be the responsibility of all the Insurance Company engaged in
this process to ensure that duplicate enrolment does not happen in the given
cluster/district and therefore shall mandatorily use AIDE App for the purpose.
Engaging companies other than L1 for enrolling non-loanee farmers is allowed for
all Districts notified and allocated by the State Governments/UT Administrations.
The enrolment of non-loanee farmers shall be as per the conditions laid down in
Para 17.7.
3.1.5 These Insurance Companies shall use digital means such as AIDE App for
enrollment of farmers and complete the process as per the seasonality discipline
detailed in Para 16.4. The same Insurance Company which has enrolled the farmers
shall be liable for the payment of claims to such farmers basis the localized crop
Operational Guidelines 2023 of PMFBY

asis the localized crop
Operational Guidelines 2023 of PMFBY

damage assessment conducted by the L1 Insurance Company or crop damage
report and crop yield data given by the concerned State Govt.
3.1.6 The exchange of information and co-witnessing of CCEs for the district by the
Government/NCIP will be limited to L1 Company only and it will be binding on other
companies to accept the yield data. The Nodal Department of State
Governments/UT Administrations shall share the yield data with all concerned
Insurance Companies for the computation of claims in the relevant IUs where they
have enrolled non-loanee farmers. Requisition for payment of Government subsidy
in respect of non-loanee farmers enrolled by the other Insurance Companies shall
be submitted directly through NCIP to the Central Nodal Agency designated by GoI
for the purpose. Claim calculation for the applications and IUs shall be done
simultaneously on NCIP based on the relevant crop-wise, IU-wise crop damage and
yield data, weather data etc. entered/shared by the respective State
Governments/UT Administrations.
3.1.7 A detailed SOP/Manual and modalities of the enrollment of non-loanee farmers at L1
premium rate by other unsuccessful bidders shall be notified by the GoI separately.
3.2 Universal Coverage
3.2.1 The States / UTs shall make all efforts for providing insurance protection to all the
farmers including tenant farmers, sharecroppers as well as loanee or non-loanee
farmers in their respective State /UT. To achieve this Universal Coverage under
PMFBY, States/UTs may adopt following approaches:
3.2.1.1 Full Universalization: Complete farmer database would be fetched on the NCIP
through the pre-validated, Land Parcel, Crop & Farmers KYC database of the
concerned State/UT. Enrolment through any other channels will not be required
under full universal coverage. The States may consider for opting full
universalization in the interest of the farmers with following pre-requisites:
 Digitized Land Records
 Farm level Season-wise Crop Data
 Farm Level KYC validated Farmers Database
 Data collection as per PMFBY Requirements
 API based Data Exchange with NCIP
3.2.1.2 Preferential Universalization: The States/UTs can incentivize the enrolment of
farmers under the scheme by subsidizing the farmers premium partially or
completely. State/UT may decide to deduct a token amount from the farmers
account during enrolment. Farmers will be enrolled through the traditional enrolment
channels under the scheme.
4. Coverage of Crops
I. Food crops (Cereals, Millets and Pulses).
II. Oilseeds.
III. Annual Commercial / Annual Horticultural crops.
IV. In addition, pilots for coverage can be taken up for those perennial
horticultural/commercial crops for which standard methodology for yield estimation

gy for yield estimation
Operational Guidelines 2023 of PMFBY

or correlation between weather condition and yield/output is available.
V. Other crops including plantations crops may also be included in the scope of scheme
subject to approval of respective ministries of Govt. of India. A separate
communication shall be issued by the GoI as and when such inclusions are required
and approved.
5. Coverage of Risks and Exclusions
5.1 Basic Cover: The basic cover under the scheme covers the risk of yield loss to
standing crops (sowing to harvesting). This comprehensive risk insurance is
provided to cover yield losses on an area-based approach basis, against non-
preventable risks like drought, dry spells, flood, inundation, wide-spread pest and
disease attack, landslides, natural fire due to lightening, storm, hailstorm, and
cyclone.
5.2 Add-On Coverage: Apart from the mandatory basic cover, the State
Governments/UT Administrations, in consultation with the State Level Coordination
Committee on Crop Insurance (SLCCCI) may choose any or all of the following add-
on covers, based on the need of the specific crop/area in their State/UT to cover the
following stages of the crop and risks leading to crop loss.
5.2.1 Prevented Sowing/Planting/Germination Risk: Insured area is prevented from
sowing/planting/germination due to deficit rainfall or adverse seasonal/climatic
conditions.
5.2.2 Mid-Season Adversity: Loss in case of adverse seasonal conditions during the crop
season viz. floods, prolonged dry spells and severe drought etc., wherein expected
yield during the season is likely to be less than 50% of the normal yield. This add-on
feature facilitates the provision for immediate relief (On- Account payment) to
insured farmers in case of occurrence of such risks.
5.2.3 Post-Harvest Losses: Coverage is available only up to a maximum period of two
weeks from harvesting, for those crops which are required to be dried in cut and
spread / small, bundled condition depending on requirement of the crops in that area
and left in the field after harvesting, against specific perils of hailstorm, cyclone,
cyclonic rains and unseasonal rains.
5.2.4 Localized Calamities: Loss/damage to notified insured crops resulting from
occurrence of identified localized risks of hailstorm, landslide, inundation, cloud burst
and natural fire due to lightening affecting isolated farms in the notified area.
5.2.5 Add-on coverage for crop loss due to attack by wild animals: The States/UTs
may consider providing add-on coverage for crop loss due to the attack by wild
animals wherever the risk is perceived to be substantial and is identifiable. The
detailed protocol and procedure for evaluation of the bids has been prepared by the
GoI in consultation with the Ministry of Environment, Forest & Climate Change
(MOEF&CC) and General Insurance Corporation of India (GIC Re). The add-on
coverage will be optional for the farmers and applicable premium, in full, shall be
borne by the farmer, however, the State Governments/UT Administrations may
consider providing additional subsidy on this coverage, wherever notified. The
Central Govt. subsidy on premium shall not be available for this add-on cover. The
actuarial premium rates for this add-on cover should be sought in the bid itself from
Operational Guidelines 2023 of PMFBY

in the bid itself from
Operational Guidelines 2023 of PMFBY

the Insurance Companies, however the add-on actuarial premium rate will be
considered separately and shall not form part of evaluation of L1. Detailed modalities
are annexed as Annexure - I for adoption by States/UTs.
5.3 States/UTs have to notify crop-wise specific period/duration for coverage of
aforesaid add-on risks in their notification as detailed in Para 7.2 of this guideline
and the same will also be uploaded on NCIP.
5.4 General Exclusions: Losses arising out of war and nuclear risks, malicious damage
and other preventable risks shall be excluded.
5.5 Loss/damage for localized calamities and post-harvest losses will be assessed at
the level of the individual insured farm and hence intimation of loss by the
farmer/designated agencies or nodal departments is essential. For remaining risks
i.e. losses due to wide-spread calamities, lodging of intimation for claims by insured
farmers / designated agencies or nodal departments for such wide-spread calamities
is not essential. Claims of wide-spread calamity shall be calculated based on the
loss assessment report submitted by the District Level Joint Committee (DLJC)
and/or actual yield submitted by concerned State Governments/UT Administrations.
5.6 Details of indemnification and claims calculation for aforesaid risk covers are
provided in Para 21.
5.7 Risk Management Mechanisms under the Scheme
5.7.1 The Scheme shall operate mainly on complete risk transfer approach wherein
complete risk is transferred to implementing Insurance Companies. This will involve
bearing of the entire claim liability by the insurance company for the discovered
premium.
5.7.2 However, if the State Governments/UT Administrations strongly believe that they
need to partake in some of the elements of the risk, they may explore opting for risk
participation approach. This shall entail adoption of alternate models by the States
wherein the claims as well as premium surplus is shared between the Government
and the implementing insurance company as per the pre-agreed formula. The States
may choose to implement any of the following models:
i. Profit & loss sharing model.
ii. Cup & Cap model (60:130)
iii. Cup & Cap model (80:110)
5.7.3 Any pilot for a model other than those listed above can be taken up in one State at
a time with the approval of Union Agriculture Minister and Union Finance Minister.
5.7.4 Following shall be the modalities specific to Risk Participation Approach:
